name: Cerebellar Ataxia
description: Hereditary cerebellar ataxia encompasses several breed-specific conditions causing progressive degeneration of the cerebellum, leading to uncoordinated movement, tremors, and balance loss. Many forms are fatal.
treatment_overview: There is no cure or disease-modifying treatment. Supportive care and physical therapy may help quality of life in slowly progressive forms. Many dogs are eventually euthanised.
prevention_tips: DNA test available for breed-specific forms
Test all breeding stock in at-risk breeds
typical_onset: Varies by breed — puppyhood to adulthood
